feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Custom field not coming in PO print xml structure
entry.content:
Dear All,

We have created a custom field and enabled it for the Purchase Order (PO) item structure, including PO user interfaces, APIs, form templates, SOAP APIs, and relevant business scenarios.

A custom form template has been developed using the data source FDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_GLO_GEN_SRV. The custom field is successfully displayed in the PO print output for Standard item category purchase orders. However, for purchase orders with the Enhanced Limit item category, the custom field is not appearing in the XML structure and consequently not reflected in the print output.

Could you please help us understand why the custom field is available for certain POs (Standard item category) but not for others (Enhanced Limit item category)? Any guidance or clarification on this behavior would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your support.

Regards

Suddy

entry.title: Requesting SAP guidance on a mobile or centralized solution to access all SAP landscape systems
entry.content:
Dear Sap Team,

We would like to inquire whether SAP provides any mobile application or centralized solution through which users can easily access all SAP landscape URLs (DEV, QAS, PRD, etc.) from a single platform.

Our higher management frequently travels and requires quick and convenient access to different SAP systems. Currently, system links are sometimes misplaced or become difficult to manage, which creates accessibility issues during travel.

Therefore, we would like to know if SAP offers:

  • A mobile application for centralized SAP landscape access
  • A secure portal/dashboard where all landscape URLs can be maintained
  • Single access point for multiple SAP environments
  • Any recommended SAP standard solution or best practice for this requirement

Kindly advise if such a solution is available and share the recommended approach from SAP side.

Thank you.

BR,

Sabtain Raza


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Technical names of project profiles in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
What are the technical names of the project profiles in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Long text in display line items in general ledger app
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We would like to know is that possible to see the long text in display line items in general ledger app.

Because for the manual docs CDS view the source data is from display line items in general ledgers "journal entry item text". But there is 50-character limitation. So, we are not receiving the full information from that header.

Whatever available in manual docs CDS view we are getting in YY1_INVOICETEXT_CDS view. when business checking the invoice texts there is inaccurate data.

or is there possible to add one column as "Notes" in display line items in general ledger app.

or how to get the long text for manual docs CDS view.

Kindly assist at earliest possible.

Thank you...
Saraswathi. S

entry.title: Free Goods Determination API which can be use for Data collect from third party system
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We have a business requirement to integrate SAP Free Goods Determination functionality with a third-party system for automated data collection and processing.

Currently, we are looking for a standard API/service that can

We would like clarification on the following points:

  1. Is there any standard SAP API available for Free Goods Determination?
  2. Are there any recommended BAPIs, function modules, or SOAP/REST APIs that can be used?
  3. What is the best practice for integrating Free Goods Determination with external systems?
  4. If no standard API exists, what enhancement or custom development approach is recommended by SAP?

entry.title: Aligning 3 SAP systems (CUSTOM, TEST, PRD) with the same data and configuration
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently working with 3 SAP systems in our landscape:

CUSTOM

TEST

PRD (production)

The issue I am facing is that production is not aligned with CUSTOM and TEST, either in terms of configuration (customizing) or data, and this is causing several problems:

Tests that pass in CUSTOM/TEST but fail in PRD.

Different parameters and customizing tables between environments.

Test data that does not reflect the reality of production.

What I would like to achieve is to align (homologate) the 3 systems in a controlled way, so that:

Configuration (customizing, roles, etc.) is aligned across the environments.

The test environment has data as close as possible to PRD (considering anonymization and compliance).

There is a clear strategy to keep this alignment over time (data refresh, homogeneous copies, transport strategy, etc.).

In our case, we understand that these activities are usually performed through a service request to SAP or to the hosting/service provider (for example, for system copies or environment refreshes). However, we are not fully clear on the exact procedure and options.

Because of that, I would like to clarify a few points:

When SAP (or the provider) performs a system copy or refresh from PRD to TEST/CUSTOM, what exactly is copied by default?

Application data only?

Customizing, repository, and technical configuration?

Security data such as users, roles, and authorizations?

If we have roles, profiles or security changes created directly in PRD, are these also replicated to the other environments during such a copy/refresh?

Or should all security and configuration changes always be transported from DEV/CUSTOM to TEST and PRD, and never created only in PRD?

My specific questions for the community are:

What is the recommended procedure to align the environments in terms of configuration and data?

Do you usually perform a full homogeneous system copy from PRD to TEST, or do you prefer a client or database refresh approach?

Is there a standard service request or request type in SAP (or in your provider’s portal) that you typically use for this kind of activity (for example “copy PRD to QAS/TEST” or “system refresh”), and what scope do you usually request (what is included/excluded)?

How do you handle comparing and aligning customizing between environments?

Do you use standard tools such as SCU0 or other comparison tools to detect differences between systems before or after the copy?

What should I consider to keep DEV/CUSTOM as a “clean” development environment (without filling it with full production data), while still having enough realistic data to test properly?

Any advice on:

A recommended sequence of steps (for example: first align customizing and roles via transports, then refresh PRD data into TEST, perform post‑copy activities, etc.).

And the typical service request / process you follow with SAP or your provider for these copies/refreshes

would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Migration Cockpit - Issue with Pricing conditions load - Q tenant
entry.content:
Whether there is any standard supported method to create Variant Keys in mass for Purchasing Pricing condition type PVA0 before migrating condition records through Migration Cockpit.

Migration Cockpit requires the VARCOND values to already exist in the target system; otherwise MAP_VARCOND cannot be confirmed and simulation fails with validation errors.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Custom Analytical Query exposed as OData V2 not available in Custom Communication Scenario
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 with the new app Custom Analytical Queries and Services.

I created a Custom Analytical Query and enabled the option:

Expose as OData V2

The query is successfully published and the activation log shows no errors.

 

Query technical name:

YY1_IMMV2_ONETOLL_EAC

However, when I try to create a Custom Communication Scenario, I cannot find this query/service under Add Services.

I also checked Display Inbound Services, but I cannot find any inbound service related to this query.

My question is:

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602, should a Custom Analytical Query exposed as OData V2 appear as an inbound service that can be added to a Custom Communication Scenario?

What I have already checked:

The query is published.
The flag Expose as OData V2 is enabled.
The activation log is successful.
The service does not appear in Custom Communication Scenarios > Add Services.
The service does not appear in Display Inbound Services.

Any guidance or official SAP documentation for the 2602 release would be appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: App against Tcode KOC4
entry.content:
Please suggest App name against Tcode KOC4 for multiple order


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Bank Statement Date in Closed Period
entry.content:
Hi Dear SAP Support,

We are facing an issue related to the processing of bank statements and would appreciate your support in resolving it.

The uploaded bank statement has a statement date of 11.12.2025. When attempting to reprocess the bank statement items, the system generates an error message indicating that the posting period is closed.

As reopening an already closed period is not a preferred option, we would like to process and post the bank statement using the currently open posting period, which is May 2026.

Could you please advise on how we can avoid these error messages and proceed with posting the bank statement in the current open period?

entry.title: Closing Journal Entries Are Missing in Generated e-Ledger TXT File – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Editi
entry.content:
Hello Community,

We are experiencing an issue in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition during the e-Ledger generation process.

When we generate the e-Ledger file via the “Run Statutory Reports” application, the closing journal entries are not included in the generated TXT file.

We have already maintained the year-end closing document type as “ZK” in the related configuration areas. However, the closing entries are still missing in the output file.

Steps to reproduce:

  1. Open the “Run Statutory Reports” application.
  2. Select the related reporting period (December 2025).
  3. Execute the e-Ledger generation process.
  4. Download the generated TXT file.
  5. Check the file content.

Actual result:
The closing journal entries are missing in the TXT output.

Expected result:
The closing journal entries should be included in the generated e-Ledger TXT file.

This is a productive system issue and critical for us, since the e-Ledger must be submitted to GİB within the legal deadline.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or found a solution for this scenario?

Thank you.

Melissa.

entry.title: Issue of adding serial numbers to sales return delivery orders via API interface
entry.content: Today I created a return sales order and a corresponding return delivery document in our system.This return sales order contains materials managed with serial numbers. I know serial numbers can be added to the delivery document via transaction code VL02N. I would like to confirm whether it is possible to add serial numbers to return delivery documents through system interfaces.

entry.title: Activation of duplicate Business Partner check using the same Tax ID (CPF and CNPJ)
entry.content: I would like to understand the configuration required to activate the validation feature that prevents the creation of a Business Partner with the same CPF or CNPJ in the Brazil localization for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

entry.title: How to check the field values for FPAYHX-REF used in payment medium
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

I am using standard CGI_CT DME structure for payment medium. in some of the nodes the soure filed is mapped as FPAYHX-REF  user defined field (eg FPAYHX-REF07, FPAYHX-REF05). but we cannot find the exact field in the system.

SO can you support to find the standard fields mapped for FPAYHX-REF.

Regards 

Kalee

entry.title: Different Supplier Details in Withholding Tax Certificate (Thailand Localization)
entry.content:
My case is that we receive supplier invoices from Company AAA and generate cheque payments to Company AAA as normal. However, the supplier requires the withholding tax certificate to be issued under Company BBB (including Company BBB’s details such as address and tax ID) instead of Company AAA.

Is it possible to specify a different supplier specifically for the withholding tax certificate in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud? Or is there any standard solution/function that can support this scenario?

entry.title: What is the data source for cost element group
entry.content:
In the Custom CDS Views app, which data source will give the details of the Cost Element Group?

What CDS View will return Cost Element Group information?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Decimal Rounding in FI invoices
entry.content:
Dear Expert , 

My client need the integer amount when calculate the tax amount in Finance invoice (incoming and Outgoing Invocie)

eg.

Net Amount = 213150

Tax amount= (213150*5%)=10657.5 (But my client want the integer amount - 10658)

Which config need to do to get this? Please advice. 

 

Thanks and Best Regards,

Hnin Oo

entry.title: I want to change the default order of the code and text for extended fields.
entry.content:
I want to change the default order of the code and text for extended fields.

The image shows journal entry data exported to Excel.

Standard fields (Supplier column) and extended fields (*Customer) are listed side by side.

The display order is reversed between the standard and extended fields: Code (Name) <=> Name (Code).

I want to unify this. Please tell me how to do this (I want the extended fields to follow the standard field order).

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Interest calcuation on Customer Open Items with 30/60/90 grace days
entry.content:
Hi,

Business requirement is to calcualte interest from Due date +30/60/90 Grace Days, but system behaviour is that when Tolerance days is configured, if the key date of calculation of interest falls within that Tolerance days, system is not calcuating interest, once the tolerance days got completed, interest is getting calculated from Net Due Date. Please assist.

entry.title: Worker Concept in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I'm researching Authorization in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and have several questions about Workers.

 1. What is the definition of a Worker in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud? Why is a Worker required in the system?

 2. What is the process for creating a Worker in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

 3. How can a Worker be removed from the system? If the Worker cannot be removed, what are the reasons?

Thank you for your answer.

QuocDT5
